title: Locale and Duration Formatting Flow
activity_flow: START: receive `seconds` (total duration as a number)
→ YES: call i18n('seconds') to get locale-aware label → return `${paddedSeconds} ${label}`
i18n_flow: Reads window.locale to select translation language
Falls back to 'en' if locale not in translations map
Looks up key in language.dictionary; falls back to 'en' dictionary
If encode_html=true: html_encode string, replace \n with
Replaces %% tokens with positional replacements array values
branch_conditions: hours === 0 && minutes === 0 → seconds-only display with i18n label
key_relationships: i18n is attached to window (global scope)
important_notes: Only the seconds-only branch invokes i18n; mm:ss and hh:mm:ss branches return raw formatted strings with no locale lookup. i18n HTML-encodes output by default. The `Service` base class (putility) is not directly involved in format_duration logic.
method_signatures: window.i18n(key: string, replacements?: array|object, encode_html?: boolean): string
